task_completion.description: Create the four required layers with appropriate colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD layer list shows 'PLATE' (White/Black), 'TICKS' (Cyan), 'LABELS' (Green), and 'DANGER' (Red).

task_completion.description: Draw the outer border and mounting hole on the PLATE layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two concentric circles centered at the origin (0,0) drawn in white/black: one large outer circle and one very small inner circle.

task_completion.description: Draw major and minor tick marks on the TICKS layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A series of cyan radial lines forming a 270-degree arc (starting bottom-left around 7 o'clock, ending bottom-right around 5 o'clock). There should be alternating longer lines (major ticks) and shorter lines (minor ticks).

task_completion.description: Add numeric labels for the major ticks on the LABELS layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Green text numbers ('0', '10', '20', ..., '100') placed radially just inside the major tick marks.

task_completion.description: Draw the danger zone arc on the DANGER layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red arc drawn along the base of the tick marks, covering only the final portion of the sweep (from the '80' label to the '100' label).

task_completion.description: Save the drawing as a DXF file in the specified location
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog or application title bar shows the file being saved as 'pressure_gauge.dxf' in the /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking on LibreCAD drawing tools, layer menus, and interacting with the canvas, rather than just running a script in the terminal.
The screenshots show the drawing being built step-by-step (e.g., circles first, then ticks, then text), proving the agent didn't just load a pre-made file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or external tool to generate the DXF file
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to execute a script that directly writes the DXF file to the destination.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The pressure gauge design requires exactly 4 layers: PLATE (White/Black), TICKS (Cyan), LABELS (Green), and DANGER (Red). The PLATE consists of an outer circle (r=50mm) and a mounting hole (r=3mm). The TICKS layer must have 21 total radial lines (11 major ticks of 5mm length, 10 minor ticks of 2.5mm length) starting from a base radius of 40mm. The sweep is exactly 270 degrees, starting at 225 degrees (bottom-left) and ending at 315 degrees (bottom-right). The LABELS layer contains text '0' through '100' in increments of 10. The DANGER layer is a red arc at r=40mm spanning exactly 54 degrees, covering the 80-100 PSI range.
